p.strong: Optimization: Coalesced reads AND writes using shared memory

p.code: output[col * height + row] = input[row * width + col]

ul.li: Read pattern: Coalesced (adjacent threads read adjacent columns in same row)
Write pattern: Strided (adjacent threads write to different rows -> non-coalesced!) Result: ~30-50 GB/s on a 760 GB/s GPU (4-6% efficiency)

div.p: Key Insight: Use shared memory as a transpose buffer

ul.li: Load from global memory with coalesced reads
Store to global memory with coalesced writes

ol.li: Coalescing: Maps thread IDs to adjacent global memory addresses.
Shared Memory: Fast on-chip scratchpad used for data rearrangement.
Padding: Prevents hardware serialization from bank conflicts.
Barriers: Ensures global memory write visibility before tile reads.

g.text: __global__ void transposeTiled(float* in, float* out, int w, int h) {

div.p: Thread 0 -> input[0] Thread 1 -> input[1] Thread 2 -> input[2] ... Adjacent threads -> Adjacent memory
Thread 0 -> output[0] Thread 1 -> output[height] Thread 2 -> output[2*height] ... Adjacent threads -> Scattered memory
Coalesced Load Read tile from global memory into shared memory ✓ Adjacent threads read adjacent addresses
Barrier Sync __syncthreads() Ensure all threads have written before any reads ⚠ Mandatory for correctness
Coalesced Write Read from shared memory with transposed indices ✓ Adjacent threads write adjacent addresses
Conflict-Free Parallelism Each row offset by 1 bank:
